title: The Revelation
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 13. Opens on the road south after the Caer Darrow escape and closes with the party still moving toward Hearthglen and Arthas after both women refuse their institutions.
openLocation: Eastweald road south of the Barov shore, late afternoon sliding toward dusk.
transport: Back on horseback after the lake crossing; southbound road ride with relay and sending-stone pauses.
immediateObjective: Deliver the Scholomance intelligence, decide what not to say about the room that produced it, and get south before the Arthas thread outruns them.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
Keep the chapter on the road south; do not cool the Caer Darrow exit into recap or stationary repair.
No clean repair of the sanctum crack; this chapter is persistence, not healing.
No explicit Mal'Ganis identification, no Jandice payoff, and no field-ready resonance weapon.

povKnowsAtOpen: Kel'Thuzad gave her the full resonance model, the animation trigger, the Lich King chain, the Burning Legion threat behind it, and the warning that his death is only a transition.
Sylvanas saw the sanctum's externals, not the argument that happened inside Jaina before Sylvanas opened the door.
Sylvanas has already filed a harder read of Jaina and is now checking where she used to trust.
Cyndia has the Farstrider relay line, and Lyriel's absence is still visible in the three-rider formation.
Hearthglen is already in crisis and Arthas is moving fast enough that the road south cannot wait for emotional cleanup.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Sylvanas will ever ask what really happened in the sanctum.
How much uglier Sylvanas's route to the sanctum was than the guided path Jandice gave Jaina.
Whether Antonidas or the Convocation can be made to move at the speed this threat requires.
Whether Arthas can still be reached before urgency hardens into something worse.
Whether Kel'Thuzad's return can be prevented rather than merely anticipated.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No clean repair of the sanctum crack.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Sylvanas now has the full strategic picture: Lich King, Burning Legion, resonance mechanism, ward failure, and Kel'Thuzad's expected lich return.
Jaina shared the intelligence product but still withheld the room, the method, and the moment of recognition.
Jaina now knows Sylvanas came through the hidden work rooms and saw the peasant stock, failed remnants, and body-part experiments her own route never touched.
Both the Convocation and Antonidas have effectively been refused in favor of the mission.
Hearthglen and Arthas are now the immediate southbound priority, and the damaged three-rider formation is still moving.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If she gives Sylvanas clean, useful intelligence and keeps the room out of it, the mission can keep moving.
pov.surfaceObjective: Deliver what Scholomance taught her without reopening the wound Scholomance left behind.
pov.pressureSource: Sylvanas's half-step, the way Sylvanas now listens for gaps, and two institutions about to demand control of what Jaina now knows.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps treating correct data as sufficient payment. If the briefing is right, the process can stay buried.
pov.chapterTest: The road asks whether Jaina can choose honest partnership over clean analysis once the analysis becomes personally compromising.
pov.forcedChoice: Explain the room and risk breaking the formation in the field, or give only the usable intelligence and carry the rest alone.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina chooses the partial truth on purpose, not by accident.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 15 opens with the intelligence shared, the omission intact, and the partnership still functioning with trust that now comes with a check attached.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Commander, listener, and a woman measuring the source even while she needs it.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Jaina reads the half-step as oversight and distrust. She cannot yet price what continued commitment is still hiding inside that same posture.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Sylvanas keeps listening, keeps moving with Jaina, and keeps treating the information as too important to ignore even while reclassifying the source.
throughPov.interactionRule: Keep Sylvanas short, controlled, and mostly external. Her listening is the pressure.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Damaged but functional three-rider formation, still moving south under time pressure.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The pair shifts from collaborative back-and-forth into formal briefing and hard listening, then chooses to keep going anyway.
team.namingAttitudeRule: No melodrama. Let the damage show through spacing, pauses, and what each woman does not ask.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This chapter is persistence, not healing.

entries.rule: Listens hard, files every gap, refuses the Convocation by continuing south, and does not offer repair language here.
Holds the left position, carries the relay stone, notices the silence, and does not try to mediate it.
Mentor voice only. Concerned, tired, and institutionally too slow, not cruel.
Voice only through the Farstrider relay. Keep the politics brief and sharp.
Off-page destination pressure only. Do not bring them on stage before Chapter 15.
Off-page loose thread. Do not spend this chapter on her.
Absent. Do not drag that clue into the road south.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 15 opens with the party reaching Hearthglen and Arthas's war camp with the full picture shared, the institutions refused, and the crack still active inside the formation.

factsLockedForChapter15: Sylvanas now knows the Lich King chain, Burning Legion threat behind it, resonance mechanism, ward-defense failure, and Kel'Thuzad's expected lich return.
Jaina still has not disclosed the room, the method of acquisition, or the moment of recognition inside Scholomance.
The Convocation recall was ignored and Antonidas's request to come home was refused.
The road formation remains Cyndia left, Jaina center, Sylvanas forward-right by half-step.
Hearthglen has already been hit, Uther is present on the road ahead, and Arthas is moving toward Stratholme.
Jandice remains an off-stage loose thread.
factsNotYetEarned: Any clean repair conversation between Jaina and Sylvanas.
Any payoff on Jandice or the old seller / repeated-farewell file.

scenes.title: The Road
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the crack from inside Jaina's saddle, then force her to decide that the briefing will cover the intelligence product and not the room that produced it.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Kel'Thuzad is dead, the intelligence is in Jaina's head, and the road south cannot wait.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina can sort the facts cleanly. She cannot sort what the room means without admitting why it reached her.

enrichment.detail: This stretch of Eastweald should still read as working Lordaeron farm country on the eve of ruin: mixed hardwood edges, orchard land, harvest fields, and mud-heavy roads carrying ordinary wagon traffic. Hearthglen sits on a fortified northern approach rather than in isolated wilderness, so even the road pressure should feel like it is driving toward a defended town already under strain.

beats.emotion: Jaina keeps the Scholomance intelligence in one mental stack and the room itself in another, because mixing them will break her control before she ever starts speaking.
beats.dilemma: Stay inside the sanctum memory, or cut it down into reportable facts that can survive the road.
beats.decision: Compartmentalize. Carry the intelligence in one stack, the room in another, and speak only from the first.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina is already dividing Scholomance into what can be reported and what cannot.
The world outside Caer Darrow feels too ordinary after hours underground.
Jaina notices the changed formation before anyone says a word.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether compartmentalizing will hold once Sylvanas starts listening.
beats.transitionIntent: emotional-carry: once Jaina has stacked the report in her head, she has to read the altered formation carrying it.
beats.verifierNotes: Eastweald is already established occupied countryside. Use familiar working-road pressure, not a fresh location introduction.

beats.goal: Ride in formation and read what changed after the sanctum.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas rides forward-right by half a horse length, close enough to hear everything and not close enough to count as level. Cyndia keeps checking the empty right flank out of habit. Nobody speaks.

outcome.summary: Jaina confirms the crack has become part of the road formation, and there is no clean field way to address it here.

newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas has shifted from easy working trust to quiet verification.
Cyndia notices the silence and leaves it alone.
The old riding rhythm between Jaina and Sylvanas is gone for now.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas will ask directly about the sanctum.
Whether the formation can carry a full briefing without cracking wider.
beats.transitionIntent: decision-carry: the changed riding line forces Jaina to decide what kind of briefing this will be.
beats.verifierNotes: Cyndia's empty-flank awareness is Lyriel-absence continuity already embedded in live drill.

beats.emotion: Jaina feels the weight of the full truth and the sharper usefulness of the partial one.
beats.dilemma: Report the intelligence and the room together, or report only the parts Sylvanas can act on immediately.
beats.decision: Give Sylvanas the intelligence product and keep the room to herself unless Sylvanas asks directly.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina knows the omission is useful and dishonest at the same time.
She frames the choice as road necessity, even while knowing cowardice is mixed into it.
The gap between what happened and what will be said is now deliberate.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas will let the clean version stand.
beats.transitionIntent: briefing-carry: once the omission is chosen, Jaina can finally start the usable report.

scenes.title: The Briefing
scenes.sceneFunction: Deliver the Scholomance intelligence cleanly, then let Sylvanas force the missing human cost back into Jaina's clean account.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina has chosen to report the product, not the process.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina can deliver the usable intelligence cleanly, but every clean sentence widens the gap around the room she is refusing to name.

beats.goal: Deliver the strategic picture: the Lich King, the Burning Legion, and the real scale of the plague.
beats.conflict: Jaina has to brief like a professional while knowing Sylvanas is now listening as both commander and evaluator of the source.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the full strategic picture, but the partnership slips into briefing mode instead of equal back-and-forth.

newOnPageInformation: The plague is part of a larger campaign directed by the Lich King from Northrend.
The Burning Legion sits above that chain instead of the plague being a purely local Lordaeron disaster.
Lordaeron is being treated as a conversion project and staging ground, not just a target.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What either woman can do with that scale before the next city falls.
How much of Sylvanas's silence is trust in the facts and how much is scrutiny of the source.
beats.transitionIntent: escalation-carry: once the larger war frame is shared, Jaina has to explain why the defenses they trusted are the wrong defenses.
beats.disclosureProvenance: The threat-chain details come from Kel'Thuzad's direct explanation in Chapter 12 plus Jaina's own cross-checking. Jaina reports the content without describing the tone or circumstances of the exchange.
beats.verifierNotes: The Lich King / Burning Legion chain is established reader knowledge; this beat hands it to Sylvanas as field intelligence.

beats.goal: Explain the plague's real mechanism and the countermeasure logic that follows from it.
beats.conflict: Jaina starts giving the clean Scholomance answer in theorem language, but Sylvanas keeps forcing the off-route rooms, the peasant-stock evidence, and the curated-route gap back into the account.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the mechanism, but Jaina is forced to admit her own route through Scholomance was curated and sanitized.

beats.valueShift: threat scale shared -> mechanism at human cost
newOnPageInformation: The plague works by resonance through living tissue, not by brute magical impact.
Force-blocking ward architecture can miss or only partly blunt that kind of attack.
Sylvanas came through rooms full of peasant stock and failed work that Jandice never showed Jaina.
Reanimated body parts were being tested separately from whole corpses.
The right answer is disruption during the buildup window, but Jaina does not yet have a broad field-ready or city-scale method — only the beginnings of a targeted source-node logic.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How to apply the countermeasure at city or kingdom scale.
Whether Jaina can defend the theory without sounding closer to Kel'Thuzad than she wants to.
beats.transitionIntent: human-cost-carry: once Sylvanas drags bodies back into the theorem, Jaina has to say Kel'Thuzad did not expect death to end him.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Jaina starts in clean mage-register theorem language; Sylvanas keeps dragging the explanation back to bodies, curated-route evidence, and what Jaina failed to see.
beats.disclosureProvenance: The mechanism comes from Kel'Thuzad's explanation and Jaina's synthesis, but Sylvanas's interruption adds the off-route rooms Jaina herself never saw.
beats.verifierModification: Clarified the countermeasure limit: Jaina lacks a broad field-ready or city-scale answer, not every possible targeted use, so chapter15's source-node plan remains earned.
beats.verifierNotes: This beat advances the already-resolved mechanism into road-useful field language. Do not frame it as a complete city-scale cure.

beats.goal: Tell Sylvanas that Kel'Thuzad expected to survive his own death as a lich.
beats.conflict: This is the part Jaina should have delivered sooner, and after Sylvanas names what the institution was doing to peasants and stock, the pause before she says it lands even harder.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas recalibrates Kel'Thuzad's death in real time, but the delay becomes another filed gap in Jaina's reporting.

beats.valueShift: mechanism at human cost -> death destabilized
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad considered bodily death a transition rather than an ending.
Crude undead continuity and high-fidelity preserved selfhood are different products of the same broader system.
The same institution treating peasants as stock reserved a higher tier of preservation for its chosen master.
Kel'Thuzad's dying calm now reads as expectation, not courage.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How a lich transition can be interrupted.
Whether Sylvanas will ever speak aloud about the reporting gap she just noticed.
beats.transitionIntent: subtext-carry: with the technical briefing complete, the pressure shifts to the thing Jaina still refuses to explain.
beats.disclosureProvenance: Kel'Thuzad's expected lich return is direct self-report from the sanctum, not outside inference.
beats.verifierNotes: Kel'Thuzad's calm around death is already established; this beat advances what that calm means strategically.

beats.emotion: Relief at having delivered the usable parts collides with the heavier weight of the parts she kept.
beats.dilemma: Correct Sylvanas's room-read now, or let the clean useful version stand.
beats.decision: Say nothing. Let the useful version hold and keep riding.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina knows Sylvanas's read is built on real external evidence, not fantasy.
Jaina's first instinct was to answer horror with theory, and she knows Sylvanas heard that instinct clearly.
The part Jaina cannot explain cleanly is recognition, not coercion.
Silence now protects the mission and injures the partnership at the same time.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this silence will remain workable once Arthas enters the field.
beats.transitionIntent: interrupt-carry: before either woman can test the silence, outside messages hit the formation.

scenes.title: The Vise
scenes.sceneFunction: Force both women to choose the mission over their institutions, then shove the chapter hard toward Hearthglen and Arthas.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The strategic briefing is done and the omission is still in place.
scenes.arcPressure: Once Sylvanas refuses her institution, Jaina has to decide whether she will do the same before either woman has repaired the crack between them.

enrichment.detail: Antonidas is not only Jaina's mentor but the Grand Magus and de facto ruler of Dalaran, so his recall carries real institutional weight even when he sounds tired and personal. Keeping him concerned, humane, and still too slow for the field matches both canon and the story's ongoing 'right mind, wrong speed' pattern.

beats.goal: Receive and process the next Farstrider relay from the north.
beats.conflict: The message is not field intelligence but institutional pressure: the Convocation is formally ordering Sylvanas home while the worst threat on the road is still south.

outcome.summary: The recall lands, but Sylvanas refuses it by not answering and not slowing down.

newOnPageInformation: The Convocation has escalated from disapproval to formal recall.
Lor'themar is delivering the order under visible political strain.
Cyndia witnesses Sylvanas's refusal as silence and continued motion rather than speech.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the long political cost of ignoring the order will be.
beats.transitionIntent: mirror-carry: once Sylvanas refuses her own institution, Jaina has to answer hers.

beats.goal: Report to Antonidas and decide whether Jaina will return to Dalaran.
beats.conflict: Antonidas is tired, worried, and far away. Jaina can give him the strategic danger, but not the whole mechanism, and not the room.

outcome.summary: Antonidas gets the warning he needs, but Jaina still withholds key context and refuses his request to come home.

newOnPageInformation: Antonidas now knows the Lich King chain and the Burning Legion threat behind it.
Jaina still does not give him the full resonance formalization or the truth of the sanctum exchange.
"Come home" lands first as Antonidas's frightened mentor plea, with Dalaran's slower institutional tempo still hanging behind it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Dalaran can do with the partial theory Jaina has given them.
Whether Antonidas will move faster now that he knows what Kel'Thuzad became part of.
beats.transitionIntent: urgency-carry: with both institutions refused, only fresh field traffic matters now.
beats.verifierModification: Refined the recall beat so Antonidas reads as mentor-first and institution-second, matching established memory instead of flattening him into generic bureaucracy.
beats.verifierNotes: Antonidas's recall should read as frightened mentor pressure first, institutional drag second.

beats.goal: Absorb the next southbound relay and set the immediate road objective.
beats.conflict: While they were underground and then briefing, the plague kept moving. Hearthglen has already taken the hit, and Arthas is not holding position.

outcome.summary: The direction is now clear, but they are already late and riding toward a town that survived only provisionally and a prince moving faster than his understanding.

newOnPageInformation: Hearthglen held only by brute force and is still actively unstable after a major plague activation.
Uther is on the field and the Silver Hand is already committed.
Arthas is moving toward Stratholme instead of consolidating where he is.
The party is no longer following a cold trail; they are chasing a live fuse.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether they can reach Arthas before urgency turns into atrocity.
How many other grain-fed sites are already too far gone.
beats.transitionIntent: motion-carry: the Hearthglen relay leaves no room for more debate and drives the chapter onto movement.
beats.verifierModification: Strengthened Hearthglen's instability so chapter15 inherits an actively failing town rather than a crisis that already feels settled.
beats.verifierNotes: Hearthglen is still unstable at chapter end; do not treat this relay as proof the town is safely held.

scenes.title: South
scenes.sceneFunction: Close the Scholomance arc on motion rather than repair and lock the damaged formation into the Arthas approach.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The full picture is shared, the institutions have been refused, and the southbound threat has overtaken every private question.

enrichment.detail: Thalassian field horses should read as fine-boned, endurance-bred patrol mounts with quiet tack, disciplined handling, and speed that feels practiced rather than flashy. That lets the final acceleration south feel elegant and hard-used at once instead of ceremonial.

beats.emotion: There is no relief, only the blunt steadiness of people who know the next crisis is already closer than the last one.
beats.dilemma: Stop and try to speak the crack aloud, or keep the damaged formation intact and ride south under necessity.
beats.decision: Keep the formation, keep the silence, and move.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina understands that being right alone is the road that leads toward Kel'Thuzad, not away from him.
Sylvanas's friction is still necessary to Jaina even after it starts hurting.
The three-rider formation still works as the shape that gets them where they need to go even though trust now comes with a check attached.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Arthas will look like when they catch him.
Whether the next arc hardens the crack or starts closing it.
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-handoff: chapter15 opens on arrival at Hearthglen and Arthas's war camp.

warnings: Beat 1 opens on internal sorting rather than immediate external action. Keep the draft anchored in saddle motion, altered formation, and road sensation so the chapter lands in motion instead of abstraction.

Beats 5-7 form a three-beat downward run — mechanism at human cost, death destabilized, omission calcified. Differentiate the pressure types sharply so the middle does not flatten into one continuous descent.

Scene-beat outcomes skew heavily yes-but: beats 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10 all land compromised success, with beat 2 as the only no-and and no clean yes. The draft will need strong cost differentiation so each success feels categorically different.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter13's extraction road: Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia are already moving south after Caer Darrow with Kel'Thuzad dead, Jandice loose, the mechanism newly won, and the sanctum misread carried into the three-rider formation. Chapter14 opens on that same road-state, converting the damaged silence into briefing pressure before Hearthglen and Arthas overtake any chance at private repair.

craftResearch.findings: Carry the damage in subtext, spacing, omission, and work dialogue instead of explicit reconciliation. Let cooperation continue while the absent conversation keeps pressure on every exchange.
craftResearch.source: Scottish Book Trust; Jane Friedman; sequel-design craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Road scenes stay alive when they do multiple jobs at once: movement, conflict, revelation, and ticking-clock escalation. Skip inert miles and keep relevant pressure active on the road itself.
craftResearch.source: Jenna Moreci; Writing Beginner; fantasy travel-scene craft articles

craftResearch.findings: Open in immediate aftermath motion, anchor place and body fast, and turn the prior escape into a new chapter question instead of recap.
craftResearch.source: Dabble in medias res guidance; chapter opening craft articles

craftResearch.findings: Close on a decision, fresh threat, or renewed race rather than on settled explanation. The reader should feel a pause-point, not comfort.
craftResearch.source: Book Light Editorial; Inspired Creative Co.; chapter-ending craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: After high-pressure reveals, characters need brief reaction-decision space so consequence lands before the next wave. Sequels work best when emotion becomes the next choice.
craftResearch.source: Not MFA sequel design; Helping Writers Become Authors; aftermath-beat guidance

pacing.notes: The chapter opens on the road and keeps multiple jobs live at once: damaged formation, withheld truth, strategic briefing, and a shrinking southbound clock. The main pacing risk is the yes-but cluster across beats 4-10, but the sequel beats at 1, 3, 7, and 11 give the chapter enough breath to prevent flattening.

structure.notes: The opening lands in immediate aftermath motion, the real mid-chapter turn hits at beat 5 when Sylvanas drags bodies back into Jaina's theorem, and the close hands directly into chapter15. The main structural caution is semantic, not architectural: beat 5 had to preserve a narrow future source plan, and beat 10 had to keep Hearthglen actively unstable for the next chapter.

character.notes: Jaina remains active throughout by choosing omission, choosing what to brief, and choosing mission over confession. Sylvanas stays fully external pressure through listening and spacing rather than POV leakage, while Cyndia remains distinct through left-flank geometry, relay handling, and her refusal to mediate the crack.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified and refined into clearer type-led carries. Scene boundaries land cleanly: scene 1 -> 2 through the chosen briefing, 2 -> 3 through interrupted silence, 3 -> 4 through relay-forced motion, and the chapter bridge directly carries chapter13's road-state into chapter14's opening.

continuity.notes: 0 new beats, 0 callbacks, 11 evolutions. The chapter mostly advances state won in chapter13: the road geometry, the Scholomance intelligence, institutional lag, and the Hearthglen / Arthas fuse. Anti-reintroduction fixes focused on preserving Antonidas as mentor-first, keeping the mechanism broader than a city-ready cure, and ensuring Hearthglen stays provisionally held rather than safely resolved.

craftResearch: Aftermath openings work best when they begin in bodily motion rather than detached summary, which matches the chapter's saddle-and-formation start.
Travel scenes need more than transit; this chapter correctly loads the road with relationship pressure, strategic briefing, and ticking-clock escalation.
Fracture scenes gain force from omission and spacing, which validates the half-step formation and the withheld sanctum truth as the chapter's main emotional engine.
Middle-chapter closings should end on renewed forward pressure, which supports the Hearthglen / Arthas relay over any private repair scene.
Reaction-decision beats are necessary after sustained tension, which makes beats 1, 3, 7, and 11 load-bearing rather than optional pauses.
structure: Beat 1 opens slightly internal, but the altered formation and road sensation keep it in motion rather than recap.
Beats 4-6 form the chapter's true turn from abstract scale into embodied cost, which keeps the briefing from reading as pure exposition.
The heavy yes-but palette across beats 4-10 is a real monotony risk unless each beat carries a different kind of cost.
Scene 3 avoids feeling like repeated briefing because institutional mirror pressure changes the conflict source from private omission to public command drag.
Beat 11 closes on a choice to keep moving, which hands cleanly to chapter15 without pretending the sanctum crack is repaired.
character: Jaina drives the chapter by making decisions in beats 1, 3, 7, 9, and 11 instead of merely receiving scrutiny.
Sylvanas remains fully filtered through Jaina's read: posture, listening, and spacing do the work without breaking limited-third absolute.
Cyndia has a distinct narrative job from both leads: formation witness, relay carrier, and silent third point rather than generic escort mass.
Antonidas and Lor'themar serve different pressure roles, keeping the institutions from blurring into one repeated obstruction beat.
The arc-position promise lands: the chapter tests whether Jaina will choose honest partnership over clean analysis, and the answer is a deliberate partial truth.
continuity: The chapter opens on the exact road-state chapter13 handed off, so the altered formation had to be treated as carried damage rather than a fresh reveal.
The Lich King chain, Burning Legion frame, and completed mechanism are established to the reader already; here they evolve by entering Sylvanas's lane.
Jandice's curated route and the hidden body-work are inherited from chapter13's breach read and therefore advance the sanctum fallout instead of reintroducing Scholomance horror.
Antonidas's “come home” continues an existing mentor line and cannot be flattened into generic Dalaran bureaucracy without losing continuity accuracy.
Hearthglen and Arthas were already live threads by chapter13, so beat 10 had to escalate them into immediate crisis instead of presenting them as new stakes.
transitions: Beat 1 hands naturally into beat 2 because the stacked report forces Jaina to read the changed line carrying it.
Beat 3 cleanly launches scene 2: once omission is chosen, the usable briefing can begin.
Beat 5 to beat 6 is the strongest carry in the chapter because putting bodies back into the mechanism forces the lich-return disclosure.
Beat 7 to beat 8 works because outside institutional pressure interrupts private silence before the silence can resolve into conversation.
Beat 10 to beat 11 is a genuine motion carry: the relay collapses debate and leaves only the choice to keep riding.
reshape: Beat 5 was clarified so the chapter preserves a future targeted source-node plan while still denying Jaina a broad city-scale fix.
Beat 9 was refined so Antonidas remains frightened mentor first and institutional lag second, preserving his established role.
Beat 10 was tightened so Hearthglen remains provisionally held and actively unstable, keeping chapter15's opening earned.
Added beat-level continuityStatus and memoryRef metadata plus a focused requiredMemory list for downstream agents.
Retyped all transitionIntent strings into clearer type-led carries and added a direct same-road chapterBridge without changing scene or beat order.

modifications.description: Beat 5 now says Jaina lacks a broad field-ready or city-scale answer, preserving chapter15's narrow source-node plan while keeping the current chapter honest about the limits.
Beat 9 now frames Antonidas's “come home” as frightened mentor pressure first, with Dalaran's slower institutional tempo behind it.
Beat 10 now preserves Hearthglen as provisionally held and actively unstable so chapter15 inherits a town still fighting rather than a crisis already cooled.
Annotated every beat as evolution and attached targeted memoryRef entries so downstream agents can read only the files this chapter truly builds on.
Retyped all transitionIntent values into clearer type-led carries so each beat names the causal or emotional pull into the next one.
Added a direct same-road chapterBridge linking chapter13's extraction state to chapter14's damaged briefing ride.
